img.compress.name: Image Compress
img.compress.seo.title: Free Online Image Compressor — Compress JPG, PNG, WebP | Tool Box Nova
img.compress.seo.desc: Free online image compressor — no server upload, no registration. Compress JPG, PNG and WebP images by up to 90% directly in your browser using Canvas API and WebAssembly. Batch compress up to 20 images at once, convert between formats (JPG/PNG/WebP/GIF/BMP), resize images by setting a max width, and download all results as a ZIP archive. Your files never leave your device — 100% private and secure. Perfect for web developers optimizing page speed, bloggers reducing image file sizes, and anyone who wants fast, free, lossless-quality image compression without installing software.

img.compress.feature.free.title: Completely Free

img.compress.desc: Compress JPG, PNG and WebP images intelligently — dramatically smaller files with visually lossless quality
img.compress.options.title: Compression Settings
img.compress.s1.label: How it works
img.compress.s1.title: Compress images in three simple steps
img.compress.s1.body: No software to install. Everything runs directly in your browser using the Canvas API — your files never leave your device.
img.compress.s1.step1.title: Upload your images
img.compress.s1.step1.desc: Drag & drop or click to select up to 20 images at once. Supports JPG, PNG, WebP, GIF, BMP, TIFF.
img.compress.s1.step2.title: Adjust settings
img.compress.s1.step2.desc: Set your preferred quality (1–100), choose an output format, or cap the maximum width. Changes apply instantly.
img.compress.s1.step3.title: Download compressed files
img.compress.s1.step3.desc: Download each image individually or grab them all in a single ZIP archive with one click.
img.compress.s2.label: Smart compression
img.compress.s2.title: Reduce file size without sacrificing quality
img.compress.s2.body1: Our browser-side compression engine intelligently removes invisible data from your images. A typical JPEG can be reduced by 40–80% while still looking crisp on any screen.
img.compress.s2.body2: The quality slider gives you full control. Slide to the left for maximum savings, or keep it high when pixel-perfect fidelity matters — like product photos or portfolio shots.
img.compress.s3.label: Format support
img.compress.s3.title: Compress & convert every major format
img.compress.s3.body: Whether you're working with photos, graphics, or web assets, we handle all common image formats. You can even convert between them during compression.
img.compress.s3.fmt.jpg: Best for photographs and complex images with many colors. Adjustable lossy compression keeps quality high while shrinking file size dramatically.
img.compress.s3.fmt.png: Lossless format ideal for graphics, logos, and images with transparency. We reduce PNG size by optimizing color depth and metadata.
img.compress.s3.fmt.webp: Google's modern format delivers 25–35% smaller files than JPEG at equivalent quality. Perfect for web pages that need fast load times.
img.compress.s3.fmt.resize.name: Resize on compress
img.compress.s3.fmt.resize.desc: Set a maximum width to automatically downscale oversized images. Aspect ratio is always preserved — no stretching or distortion.
img.compress.s4.label: 100% private
img.compress.s4.title: Your images never leave your browser
img.compress.s4.body1: Unlike most online image tools, we never upload your files to a server. Every byte of processing happens locally using the browser's Canvas API and WebAssembly.
img.compress.s4.body2: This means your personal photos, confidential documents, or proprietary design assets stay completely private — always. No account required, no data stored, no tracking of your files.
img.compress.s5.label: Batch processing
img.compress.s5.title: Compress up to 20 images at once
img.compress.s5.body1: No need to compress images one by one. Drop a whole folder of images and compress them all simultaneously. When you're done, download everything in a single ZIP file — saving time on projects big and small.
img.compress.s5.body2: Each image in the batch shows its individual compression ratio and the exact bytes saved, so you always know what you're getting. Re-compress with different settings anytime without re-uploading.
img.compress.faq.title: Frequently asked questions
img.compress.s6.label: About this tool
img.compress.s6.title: The best free online image compressor — fast, private, and powerful
img.compress.s6.body1: This free online image compressor lets you reduce the file size of JPG, PNG, WebP, GIF, and BMP images directly in your browser — no upload to any server required. Whether you need to compress photos for a website, optimize images for faster page loading, shrink attachments before sending by email, or batch-process hundreds of product images, this tool handles it all instantly without any software installation.
img.compress.s6.body2: Unlike cloud-based tools like TinyPNG or Squoosh that send your files to remote servers, all compression here runs 100% locally using the browser's Canvas API and WebAssembly technology. Your images never leave your device — making this the safest choice for sensitive photos, confidential documents, or proprietary design assets. There is no registration, no file-size quota, and no daily limit.
img.compress.s6.body3: Key features include: batch compression of up to 20 images at once, adjustable quality slider (1–100%) for full control over file size vs. visual fidelity, one-click format conversion between JPG, PNG, and WebP, automatic resizing by setting a maximum width, and ZIP download for all compressed results. Start compressing your images for free — just drag, drop, and download.

img.resize.feature.privacy.title: Pixel-Perfect Accuracy
img.resize.feature.privacy.desc: Resize by exact pixels, percentage, or choose from 12+ social media presets. Aspect ratio lock ensures distortion-free results every time.
img.resize.feature.batch.title: Multi-Format Output
img.resize.feature.batch.desc: Export resized images as JPG, PNG, or WebP with an adjustable quality slider. Convert formats while resizing in a single step.
img.resize.feature.preset.title: Instant & Secure
img.resize.feature.preset.desc: Powered by the browser's Canvas API — results appear in under a second with no waiting, no uploads, and no privacy risk.
img.resize.faq.title: FAQ
img.resize.about.label: About
img.resize.about.title: The most powerful free image resizer — resize JPG, PNG, WebP online
img.resize.about.body1: Need to resize images for a website, social media campaign, email newsletter, or print project? This free online image resizer handles every scenario with professional precision — directly in your browser, with no software to install and no account required. Simply drag and drop your JPG, PNG, or WebP files, set your target dimensions, and download the resized images in seconds.
img.resize.about.body2: Three resize modes give you complete flexibility. In Pixel mode, enter exact width and height values from 1 to 10,000 pixels — perfect when you need a specific canvas size for a web banner or print template. In Percentage mode, scale images from 10% to 200% of their original size with a smooth slider, ideal for quick size reductions or upscaling tests. In Preset mode, choose from 12 built-in social media templates: Instagram Post (1080×1080), Instagram Story (1080×1920), Facebook Cover (820×312), YouTube Thumbnail (1280×720), Twitter/X Post (1200×675), LinkedIn Post (1200×627), Full HD (1920×1080), 4K (3840×2160), and more — all updated to match each platform's current recommended dimensions.
img.resize.about.body3: The aspect ratio lock prevents unwanted distortion: enable it and changing one dimension automatically updates the other to maintain the original proportions. Disable it to set completely independent width and height values for creative crops. After resizing, choose your output format — keep the original, convert to JPG for maximum web compatibility, switch to PNG for lossless quality, or pick WebP for the smallest file size. A quality slider (20–100%) lets you fine-tune the file size vs. visual quality trade-off for JPG and WebP outputs.
img.resize.about.body4: The tool is powered entirely by the browser's Canvas API, which means all processing happens locally on your device — no files are ever transmitted to a server, eliminating both upload waiting time and privacy concerns. This makes it safe to resize confidential business images, personal photos, or sensitive documents without worry. Results appear in under a second for most images, even at maximum resolution.
img.resize.about.body5: Whether you are a web developer optimising page load speeds, a social media manager preparing platform-specific assets, an e-commerce seller standardising product photos, or a designer prepping files for a client presentation — this tool saves hours of repetitive work every week. It works on Windows, Mac, iOS, Android and any modern browser. No plugins, no subscriptions, no watermarks on output. Just resize, download, and move on.
img.metadata.name: Image Metadata Viewer
img.metadata.seo.title: Image Metadata Viewer — Read EXIF, GPS, IPTC Data Online Free | Tool Box Nova
img.metadata.seo.desc: Free online image metadata viewer. Read EXIF, GPS location (map preview), IPTC copyright, and XMP data. Files never leave your browser. Export as JSON, CSV or TXT.
img.metadata.hero.title: Read Hidden Image Metadata Online
img.metadata.hero.subtitle: View EXIF camera settings, GPS map location, IPTC copyright info. Files stay in your browser.
img.metadata.upload.title: Drop images here
img.metadata.upload.hint: JPG / PNG / WebP / TIFF / HEIC · Max 20MB each · Up to 10 files
img.metadata.upload.multi: Drop multiple images to compare their metadata
img.metadata.no_gps: No GPS data in this image
img.metadata.error.unsupported: Unsupported format. Use JPG, PNG, WebP, TIFF or HEIC.
img.metadata.error.too_large: File too large. Max 20MB per image.
img.metadata.error.parse: Failed to parse metadata. The image may not contain EXIF data.
img.metadata.feature.privacy.title: Six Data Categories
img.metadata.feature.privacy.desc: Extracts and organises Basic Info, Camera Settings, GPS Location, IPTC Copyright, XMP tags, and raw binary data — all in clearly labelled sections.
img.metadata.feature.gps.title: Precise Location Mapping
img.metadata.feature.gps.desc: GPS coordinates are plotted on an interactive OpenStreetMap with latitude, longitude, and altitude — plus a direct link to open the spot in your maps app.
img.metadata.feature.export.title: Developer-Ready Export
img.metadata.feature.export.desc: Export structured JSON for APIs, CSV for spreadsheets, or human-readable TXT reports. Download all results as a ZIP for archiving.
img.metadata.faq.title: FAQ
img.metadata.about.label: About
img.metadata.about.title: Read hidden EXIF, GPS, IPTC and XMP data from any image — free online
img.metadata.about.body1: Every digital photo contains invisible metadata that tells the full story behind the image: which camera and lens captured it, the exact settings used, when and where it was taken, who owns the copyright, and what software last processed it. This free online image metadata viewer extracts all of that information — EXIF, GPS, IPTC and XMP — directly in your browser from JPG, PNG, WebP, TIFF and HEIC files, with no file upload to any external server.
img.metadata.about.body2: For photographers and photo archivists, EXIF data reveals the complete technical story: camera make and model, ISO sensitivity, aperture (f-stop), shutter speed, focal length, flash status, white balance, and the exact date and time the shot was taken. Lens model and serial number data helps track equipment across thousands of images. This information is invaluable for culling sessions, building portfolios, and understanding which camera settings produce the best results in different conditions.
img.metadata.about.body3: For privacy-conscious users and journalists, GPS coordinates embedded in smartphone photos can expose sensitive locations — home addresses, workplace locations, visited medical facilities. This tool lets you inspect GPS data before sharing images publicly, helping you make informed decisions about which images to share and which to scrub. For GPS-tagged images, an interactive OpenStreetMap preview shows the precise shooting location at a glance.
img.metadata.about.body4: IPTC metadata is the professional standard for copyright and editorial information: author name, copyright notice, image description, keywords, city, country, and usage rights. Photo agencies, stock libraries, and digital asset management systems rely on IPTC fields to track ownership and licensing. XMP data, originally developed by Adobe, carries workflow information like star ratings, colour labels, editing software history, and colour space — essential for cross-application workflows in Lightroom, Capture One and similar tools.
img.metadata.about.body5: Process up to 10 images simultaneously and switch between them in a sidebar list to compare metadata side by side — ideal for auditing large photo libraries or verifying consistent settings across a shoot. Export metadata in JSON format for developer integrations, CSV for spreadsheet analysis in Excel or Google Sheets, or plain TXT for quick human-readable reports. Download all results as a single ZIP archive. Because all processing happens locally using the exifr.js library, your images — including sensitive GPS coordinates and personal information — never leave your device.
